title: campanula baumgartenii subsp. beckiana
intro: Campanula baumgartenii subsp. beckiana, also known as a plant species in the Campanulaceae family, is a type of bellflower native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. Its health benefits include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, Campanula baumgartenii subsp. beckiana is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
